originalBook: That death is penal, and had its origin in Adam’s sin.
title: Of the Fall of the First Man, Through Which Mortality Has Been Contracted.

source.title: NPNF1-02. St. Augustine's City of God and Christian Doctrine

verses.text: Having disposed of the very difficult questions concerning the origin of our world and the beginning of the human race, the natural order requires that we now discuss the fall of the first man (we may say of the first men), and of the origin and propagation of human death.
For God had not made man like the angels, in such a condition that, even though they had sinned, they could none the more die.

He had so made them, that if they discharged the obligations of obedience, an angelic immortality and a blessed eternity might ensue, without the intervention of death; but if they disobeyed, death should be visited on them with just sentence—which, too, has been spoken to in the preceding book.
